steps.title: Determine the value of k using the y-intercept property
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. Since the y-intercept is $(k-5, b)$, we need $k - 5 = 0$, which gives $k = 5$.
Substituting $k = 5$ into the table, the two points are $(5, 13)$ and $(5+7, -15) = (12, -15)$.

steps.title: Find the y-coordinate of the y-intercept
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with point $(5, 13)$ and slope $-4$:
$$y - 13 = -4(x - 5)$$
At the y-intercept where $x = 0$:
$$y - 13 = -4(0 - 5) = 20$$
$$y = 33$$
Therefore, $b = 33$.
